Evidence I have from the merged results follows.

Search query used (exact):
1. "artificial intelligence regulation news past 12 months OR \"AI regulation\" site:(reuters.com OR nytimes.com OR washingtonpost.com OR ft.com OR theguardian.com OR politico.com OR bloomberg.com OR axios.com OR cnn.com OR bbc.com)" (from the search action result)

Visited pages (from merged results) in order:
1) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:In_the_news/Posted/Archives

The merged-result visit content does not contain any specific news article headline, publication date, main topic about AI regulation, or a non-Wikipedia source website name.

2) URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Search
   - Page title (exact): Search - Wikipedia
   - H1 text (exact): Search

   - Notes: This is Wikipedia's search page (Special:Search), not a news article on a news website. The merged-result visit content does not contain any specific news article headline, publication date, main topic about AI regulation, or a non-Wikipedia source website name.

Extracted facts required by the mandate (explicit listing):
- Headlines of news articles about 'artificial intelligence regulation' from the past year: MISSING in the provided evidence (no news article headlines present).
- Publication dates of such articles: MISSING in the provided evidence.
- Main topics / key points of such articles: MISSING in the provided evidence.
- Source website names (non-Wikipedia news websites) for such articles: MISSING in the provided evidence.

URLs I visited (in order):
1. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:In_the_news/Posted/Archives
2. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Search

What is missing (explicit):
- The merged results do not include any visited pages from actual news websites (e.g., reuters.com, nytimes.com, ft.com, theguardian.com, politico.com, bloomberg.com, axios.com, cnn.com, bbc.com). Next steps I can take (if you want me to continue):

merged_results.title: Synthesize and compare perspectives across visited articles

result.title: Synthesize and compare perspectives across visited articles

details.intent: Using the structured outputs from the two visited articles, produce a concise comparative summary that highlights agreements, differences, emphases, quoted language, and any policy recommendations or tone differences.
input_required: article_1: {headline, pub_date, source, key_point_summary, exemplar_quote, url}
article_2: {headline, pub_date, source, key_point_summary, exemplar_quote, url}
measurable_output_fields: 3-5 bullet comparison points (e.g., focus, tone, stakeholders emphasized, regulatory approach, urgency)
verification_steps: Cite the exact headline and publication date for each article in the summary.
Include at least one direct quote per article to support claimed perspective differences.
details.justification: A focused synthesis is needed to meet the mandate's final deliverable: a summary comparing different perspectives. This node consolidates the extracted facts into an interpretable comparison for the user.
details.goal: Synthesize and compare perspectives across visited articles
details.original_goal: Synthesize and compare perspectives across visited articles
details.parent_goal: Search for recent news articles about 'artificial intelligence regulation' from the past year. Visit at least 2 different news websites (not Wikipedia) and extract: (1) The headline of each article, (2) The publication date, (3) The main topic or key point, (4) The source website name. Provide a summary comparing the different perspectives.

result.thinking_content: Synthesize and compare perspectives across visited articles

015a03ce-1773-4bcc-b237-f8d59531f19f.title: Search recent news (past 12 months) for 'artificial intelligence regulation'

details.query: artificial intelligence regulation news past 12 months OR "AI regulation" site:(reuters.com OR nytimes.com OR washingtonpost.com OR ft.com OR theguardian.com OR politico.com OR bloomberg.com OR axios.com OR cnn.com OR bbc.com)
details.intent: Locate news articles (not Wikipedia) from major news outlets published within the last 12 months about AI regulation/policy. Return a ranked set of candidate article URLs and snippets to feed the visit nodes.
verification_steps: Ensure each URL is from a news website (exclude Wikipedia, blogs, research papers).
Confirm publication date in snippet or on-visit is within the past 12 months (relative to run date).
Prefer diversity of domains; flag if top N results concentrated on one domain.
details.justification: A targeted search is required to find candidate news articles. This reduces uncertainty by producing exact URLs and result snippets that the visit nodes will open to extract authoritative full-text facts. The search will also identify domain diversity so the visits meet the 'at least 2 different news websites' requirement.
details.notes: Vector DB (links_*) will be auto-queried for related links as part of the search step to improve recall of recent news URLs.
details.goal: Search recent news (past 12 months) for 'artificial intelligence regulation'
details.original_goal: Search recent news (past 12 months) for 'artificial intelligence regulation'
details.parent_goal: Search for recent news articles about 'artificial intelligence regulation' from the past year. Visit at least 2 different news websites (not Wikipedia) and extract: (1) The headline of each article, (2) The publication date, (3) The main topic or key point, (4) The source website name. Provide a summary comparing the different perspectives.

action_result.intent: Locate news articles (not Wikipedia) from major news outlets published within the last 12 months about AI regulation/policy. Return a ranked set of candidate article URLs and snippets to feed the visit nodes.
vector_context: Synthesize and compare perspectives across visited articles

da70c3e6-ab11-4386-9afc-3b0291bd3dd8.title: Visit top search article (primary news site) and extract structured fields

details.optional_url: <use top-ranked search result URL produced by previous Search node (search_results[0])>
details.intent: Open the top search result (primary news article) and extract structured metadata and main points.
target_facts: main topic / key point (1-2 sentence summary)
one representative quote or sentence demonstrating the article's stance
extraction_plan: Load full article page (up to 6000 chars of text extraction).
Extract the headline from <title> or article header.
Find publication date and convert to standard format.
Read lead paragraphs and conclusion to synthesize the 'main topic/key point' in 1-2 sentences.
Record the source/publisher name and exact article URL.
Capture a short quote (<= 200 characters) that exemplifies the article's perspective.
measurable_output_fields: article: {url, headline, pub_date, source, author, key_point_summary, exemplar_quote}
verification_steps: Confirm pub_date is within last 12 months; if not, flag and skip.
If the chosen URL is a non-news domain or a paywall prevents extraction, fall back to the next highest-ranked search result from a different news domain.
details.justification: Visiting the full article is necessary to get authoritative headline, date, and the article's central claim. This produces verifiable evidence (quote + metadata) rather than relying on search snippets.
details.domain_uniqueness_rule: Prefer top result. If the top result's domain duplicates the second planned visit domain, pick the next search result with a different news domain.
details.goal: Visit top search article (primary news site) and extract structured fields
details.original_goal: Visit top search article (primary news site) and extract structured fields
details.parent_goal: Search for recent news articles about 'artificial intelligence regulation' from the past year. Visit at least 2 different news websites (not Wikipedia) and extract: (1) The headline of each article, (2) The publication date, (3) The main topic or key point, (4) The source website name. Provide a summary comparing the different perspectives.

faa31442-e6d2-4383-a31f-9dd17f24c8ef.title: Visit second search article on a different news website and extract structured fields

details.optional_url: <use next highest-ranked search result with a different domain than visit node 1 (search_results where domain != search_results[0].domain)>
details.intent: Open a second, domain-distinct news article from the search results and extract the same structured metadata and main points for comparison.
target_facts: main topic / key point (1-2 sentence summary)
one representative quote or sentence demonstrating the article's stance
extraction_plan: Visit the designated URL and extract headline, pub_date, author, and lead/summary sentences.
Synthesize the article's key point in 1-2 sentences.
Store the canonical URL and capture one quote that illustrates the perspective.
measurable_output_fields: article: {url, headline, pub_date, source, author, key_point_summary, exemplar_quote}
verification_steps: Confirm article pub_date within last 12 months. If paywalled or extraction fails, select the next highest-ranked result from a different domain.
Ensure the domain differs from Visit node 1 to satisfy the 'at least 2 different news websites' requirement.
details.justification: A second, independent news source is required to compare perspectives and identify consensus or divergence on AI regulation topics. Extracting the same structured fields enables direct comparison.
details.note_on_parallelism: This visit is independent of the other visit and can run in parallel after the Search node completes.
details.goal: Visit second search article on a different news website and extract structured fields
details.original_goal: Visit second search article on a different news website and extract structured fields
details.parent_goal: Search for recent news articles about 'artificial intelligence regulation' from the past year. Visit at least 2 different news websites (not Wikipedia) and extract: (1) The headline of each article, (2) The publication date, (3) The main topic or key point, (4) The source website name. Provide a summary comparing the different perspectives.

details.justification: A focused synthesis is needed to meet the mandate's final deliverable: a summary comparing different perspectives. This node consolidates the extracted facts into an interpretable comparison for the user.
